rationale.R1.2: The draft asserts a convergence proof via bounded-variance gradients and Lagrange contraction; logical consistency of these theoretical claims is central to any substantive improvement and is testable from the text alone.
rationale.R1.3: Three quantitative empirical claims are made (38% cost reduction, 17% vs BERT-router, <1% quality gap) but no table, figure, or citation anchors them; alignment between claims and stated evidence is a plausible core responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: Load-bearing: 'improve this draft' always implicates sentence- and paragraph-level prose quality; failure to improve writing invalidates the delegation regardless of any other work done.

parsed.clarification_question: Should improvements be limited to prose and writing quality only, or should they also cover the technical content — including checking the logical validity of the convergence argument and the alignment between the empirical claims and their supporting evidence?
